'''Ando''' was a [[Star/Legends|star]] in the [[Star system/Legends|star system]] of the [[Ando system/Legends|same name]], located within the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]] region of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. The star was [[Orbit/Legends|orbited]] by three [[Planet/Legends|planets]]: [[Andando]], [[Ando/Legends|Ando]] with its two [[Moon/Legends|moons]], and [[Ando Prime/Legends|Ando Prime]]. The Ando system also contained a [[Unidentified Ando system planetoid|small planetoid]] and an [[Unidentified asteroid field (Ando system)|asteroid field]].

Ando appeared from the surface of Ando Prime to be [[Color/Legends|yellow]] in color. The star was visible in the [[Atmosphere/Legends|skies]] of Ando Prime when the monks of the mystic [[Order of Dai Bendu]] [[Tho Yor Arrival|entered]] the [[Tho Yor]] [[Starship/Legends|starship]] that was resting on the world. [[Time/Legends|Millennia]] later, Ando was observed again on several instances from the planets of Ando and Ando Prime as well as the namesake system's [[Planetoid/Legends|planetoid]].

==Description==
Ando was a [[Star/Legends|star]] in the [[Star system/Legends|star system]] of the [[Ando system/Legends|same name]],<ref name="wizards" /> located within the [[Lambda sector/Legends|Lambda sector]], a part of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy's]] regions known as the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]]<ref name="EAOC" /> and [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]].<ref name="TEA" /> The star was [[Orbit/Legends|orbited]] by three [[Planet/Legends|planets]]: the [[Magma/Legends|molten rock]] world [[Andando]],<ref name="wizards" /> blasted barren and uninhabitable as a result of a [[Aqualish Civil War|civil war]] among the [[Aqualish/Legends|Aqualish]] [[Species/Legends|species]];<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|I|30|Ando}}</ref> the [[Waterworld/Legends|oceanic planet]] [[Ando/Legends|Ando]] together with its two [[Moon/Legends|moons]]; and the frozen world [[Ando Prime/Legends|Ando Prime]].<ref name="wizards" /> The Ando system also contained a [[Unidentified Ando system planetoid|small planetoid]] with a single [[Unidentified moon (Ando system)|moon]] that was located near the inner edge of an [[Unidentified asteroid field (Ando system)|asteroid field]].<ref name="Almas" /> Ando appeared from the surface of Ando Prime to be [[Color/Legends|yellow]] in color,<ref name="DotJ">[[Dawn of the Jedi: Force Storm 1|''Dawn of the Jedi: Force Storm'' 1]]</ref> and the sunlight reflected from the [[Glass/Legends|glass]]-like surface of Ando's [[Ocean/Legends|oceans]] was highly intense.<ref name="Journal11">{{JournalCite|11|The Ando Project}}</ref>

==History==
[[File:Ando_NEGAS.jpg|thumb|left|200px|The star Ando was orbited by the planet of the same name.]]
Around [[36,453 BBY]], the star Ando emerged from the clouds in the [[Atmosphere/Legends|sky]] of Ando Prime when the [[Tho Yor]] [[Starship/Legends|starship]], which had been resting on the planet's surface for some [[Time/Legends|time]], [[Tho Yor Arrival|beckoned]] the local monks of the mystic [[Order of Dai Bendu]] to enter the vessel.<ref name="DotJ" /> [[Millennium/Legends|Millennia]] later, while the [[Republic Military/Legends|Republic Military]] was [[Mission to the Ando system|attacking]] a [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatist]] [[Separatist Ando system base|base]] in the Ando system during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], the namesake star dimly illuminated the remote [[Planetoid/Legends|planetoid]] on which the base was located.<ref name="Almas" /> Around the time of the [[Battle of Ando Prime|battle]] on Ando Prime during the same conflict, the star was seen during its sunset on the [[Water/Legends|snow]]-covered world.<ref name="Lost Legion">''[[The Clone Wars: Decide Your Destiny: The Lost Legion]]''</ref>

In the following [[Standard decade/Legends|decades]], Ando was observed on several occasions from its namesake planet. Two such instances happened during the rule of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. One of them was when the Aqualish [[Myinyar]] rendezvoused with a [[Unidentified female Human (Ando)|Human female]], who wanted to purchase the rare alloy [[Sedrellium/Legends|sedrellium]] from Myinyar.<ref name="Courier's Deal">{{WEGCite|story=Courier's Deal|book=Creatures of the Galaxy}}</ref> The other sighting was when the [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] vessel [[Majestic (pirate ship)|''Majestic'']] [[Skirmish aboard the Nanda's Luck|attacked]] the fishing ship ''[[Nanda's Luck]]''.<ref name="wizards" /> In addition, when the ''[[Gambit]]'', a [[Luxury 2800|space yacht]] carrying a team of [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] agents, approached Ando and entered its atmosphere, the agents were nearly blinded by the bright reflection of Ando's sun in its oceans.<ref name="Journal11" /> In [[26 ABY/Legends|26 ABY]],<ref name="TERC">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of ''[[The New Jedi Order: Edge of Victory I: Conquest]]'' to [[26 ABY/Legends|26 ABY]].</ref> the sun of Ando was seen by the [[Khommite/Legends|Khommite]] [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Dorsk 82]] while he was protecting a crowd of [[Droid/Legends|droids]] in the [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Imthitill]] from a mob of the local Aqualish wanting to destroy them.<ref name="Conquest">''[[The New Jedi Order: Edge of Victory I: Conquest]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:CreaturesOfTheGalaxy.png|thumb|right|200px|The star Ando was introduced in ''Creatures of the Galaxy''.]]
The star Ando first appeared in "[[Courier's Deal]]," a short companion narrative published in the [[1994]] sourcebook ''[[Creatures of the Galaxy]]'' that served as a supplement to [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="Courier's Deal" /> Ando was later depicted in the [[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]] web supplement "[[Ando: Planet of the Walrus Men]]," which was published on the [[Wizards of the Coast]] [[Wizards.com|website]] in [[July]] [[2003]] and provided the star's proper name.<ref name="wizards" />

"[[Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are]]," a [[1990]] [[roleplaying game]] source article published in [[Voyages SF 13|''Voyages SF'' 13]], placed the Ando system, and therefore the star Ando, in the "Imperial Core" [[Sector/Legends|sector]]. Since that article was released outside of the [[Lucas Licensing]] process,<ref name="Twinkle">{{VoyagesCite|13|Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are}}</ref> it was considered non-[[canon]]ical with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity.<ref name="UnlicensedStatus">{{UnlicensedStatus}}</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' subsequently placed the Ando system in grid square Q-15, relatively far from the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy's]] [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]],<ref name="TEA" /> and the [[StarWars.com]] [[Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion|Online Companion]] to the book further overrode the ''Voyages SF'' 13 placement by establishing that the system was situated in the Lambda sector.<ref name="EAOC" />
